title: Calycosia lageniformis
taxonomy: Calycosia lageniformis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Calycosia, which is a group of plants native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. Calycosia lageniformis is specifically categorized as a shrub or small tree, and it is a member of the family Rubiaceae, which includes over 600 genera of flowering plants. This plant is native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands, where it can be found growing in shaded areas and along streams. Calycosia lageniformis is also known as the "Pacific island Calycosia" due to its limited geographic range.

intro: Calycosia lageniformis, also known as the Hawaiian passionflower or wild passionflower, is a plant species native to Hawaii and other Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including the treatment of anxiety, insomnia, and inflammation due to its sedative and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Calycosia lageniformis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Calycosia lageniformis include teas, tinctures, and extracts, which are used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
